Tackle complex challenges, analyze data, write code, and think through your hardest work." data-router-head=""><meta name="theme-color" content="hsl(60,2%,9%)"><style data-skill-arg-hint-shim="">
.ProseMirror [data-skill-arg-hint] ~ .ProseMirror-separator,

  display: none;
}</style><title>Crafting a recruiter-ready GitHub profile for AI/ML roles - Claude</title><meta name="x-route-title" data-router-head="" data-intended-title="Claude"><style data-image-preview-vt="">
::view-transition-group(chat-image-preview) {
  animation-duration: 350ms;
  /* CDS --cds-ease-snap */
  animation-timing-function: cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1);
}
html.vt-image-preview-closing::view-transition-group(chat-image-preview) {
  animation-duration: 250ms;
}
::view-transition-image-pair(chat-image-preview) {
  isolation: auto;
  overflow: clip;
  animation: vt-image-preview-decoration-in 350ms cubic-bezier(0.32, 0.72, 0, 1) both;
}
/* Separate close keyframes (not animation-direction: reverse) so the
   timing-function isn't time-reversed too — a reversed snap curve holds at
   full shadow then drops to zero in the last few frames, which reads as a
   jump. */
html.vt-image-preview-closing::view-transition-image-pair(chat-image-preview) {
  animation-name: vt-image-preview-decoration-out;
  animation-duration: 250ms;
}
/* Border-radius morphs between the source thumbnail's radius and the viewer's
   radius (both read from computed style at transition time and set on CSS
   custom properties) so there's no corner snap at either end. Shadow fades
   in/out alongside. */
@keyframes vt-image-preview-decoration-in {
  from {
    box-shadow: 0 4px 32px hsl(var(--always-black)/0%), 0 0 0 0.5px hsl(var(--always-black)/0%);
    border-radius: var(--vt-image-preview-source-radius, 0.5rem);
  }
  to {
    box-shadow: 0 4px 32px hsl(var(--always-black)/30%), 0 0 0 0.5px hsl(var(--always-black)/25%);
    border-radius: var(--vt-image-preview-target-radius, 0.375rem);
  }
}
@keyframes vt-image-preview-decoration-out {
  from {
    box-shadow: 0 4px 32px hsl(var(--always-black)/30%), 0 0 0 0.5px hsl(var(--always-black)/25%);
    border-radius: var(--vt-image-preview-target-radius, 0.375rem);
  }
  to {
    box-shadow: 0 4px 32px hsl(var(--always-black)/0%), 0 0 0 0.5px hsl(var(--always-black)/0%);
    border-radius: var(--vt-image-preview-source-radius, 0.5rem);
  }
}
::view-transition-old(chat-image-preview),
::view-transition-new(chat-image-preview) {
  animation: none;
  mix-blend-mode: normal;
  width: 100%;
  height: 100%;
  object-fit: cover;
}
/* Direction-aware: on open show only NEW (full image revealing); on close show
   only OLD (full image cropping back). */
html:not(.vt-image-preview-closing)::view-transition-old(chat-image-preview) {
  display: none;
}
html.vt-image-preview-closing::view-transition-new(chat-image-preview) {
  display: none;
}
/* Thumbnail off-screen on close: no destination to morph to, so fade the
   group in place instead of freezing. (Don't touch transform — the group's
   transform is its absolute position; overriding it would fling the image
   toward (0,0).) */
html.vt-image-preview-no-origin::view-transition-group(chat-image-preview) {
  animation-name: vt-image-preview-fade-out;
}
@keyframes vt-image-preview-fade-out {
  to { opacity: 0; }
}
</style></head><body class="bg-bg-100 text-text-100 font-ui min-h-screen chat-ui-core" style="pointer-events: auto;"><div id="root"><div class="cds-root text-primary contents" data-density="comfortable" data-mode="dark" data-platform="web" data-font="anthropic" style="font-size: var(--cds-font-size-body);"><div class="root"><div class="grid w-full overflow-hidden print:!h-auto print:overflow-visible print:block" style="height: calc(100dvh - var(--app-install-banner-height, 0px) - var(--dev-dashboard-height, 0px) - var(--desktop-org-banner-height, 0px) - var(--stale-session-redirect-guard-height, 0px) - var(--session-expiry-banner-height, 0px)); grid-template-rows: 0px 1fr; transition: grid-template-rows 0.15s ease-out;"><div></div><div class="flex min-h-0 min-w-0 w-full overflow-x-clip relative overflow-y-auto [scrollbar-gutter:stable] print:!overflow-visible"><div class="pointer-events-none absolute inset-0 bg-bg-100 [background-image:linear-gradient(to_right,hsl(var(--bg-200))_1px,transparent_1px),linear-gradient(to_bottom,hsl(var(--bg-200))_1px,transparent_1px)] [background-size:32px_32px]" aria-hidden="true" style="clip-path: circle(150% at 50% 50%); opacity: 0;"></div><div class="shrink-0 print:hidden" style="overflow-x: clip; width: auto; opacity: 1;"><div class="fixed lg:sticky z-sidebar" style="width: 18rem;"><div id="tally-sidebar-card" class="tally-card">

data-testid="user-message" class="font-large !font-user-message py-0.5 grid grid-cols-1 gap-2 relative [&amp;_ul]:!space-y-0 [&amp;_ol]:!space-y-0 [&amp;_ul]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:pl-8" style="max-height: none; overflow: hidden; position: relative;"><p class="whitespace-pre-wrap break-words">I am Rahul Gupta, MCA student at KNIT Sultanpur. GitHub: github.com/Rahulgupta-63. I have these completed projects: (1) Titanic ML — Random Forest 82% accuracy, (2) California Housing — R2=0.81, (3) MNIST CNN — 99.10% accuracy PyTorch, (4) RAG Study Assistant — deployed on HuggingFace Spaces at huggingface.co/spaces/rahul-63/rag-study-assistant, (5) NayePankh AI Chatbot — deployed on HuggingFace Spaces, (6) FastAPI REST API for RAG — containerized with Docker. My stack: Python, FastAPI, Docker, AWS EC2, ChromaDB, LangChain, HuggingFace, Groq API, Streamlit. Target: 8-12 LPA AI/ML engineer at Indian startups, off-campus. Help me: (1) write a profile README.md that's unique and not generic, (2) write README for each project repo with recruiter-friendly structure, (3) fix my bio, (4) suggest what to pin. Be direct, no fluff. opacity: 1; transform: none;"><div class="contents"><div data-is-streaming="false" class="group relative relative pb-[var(--msg-assistant-pb,0.75rem)]" style="opacity: 1; transform: none;"><h2 data-find-omitted="" class="sr-only select-none">Claude responded: Problem first: your project list is the real issue, not the README wording.</h2><div class="font-claude-response relative leading-[1.65rem] [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:bg-bg-000/50 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-0.5 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-border-400 [&amp;_.ignore-pre-bg&gt;div]:bg-transparent [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8"><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Problem first: your project list is the real issue, not the README wording. Six projects, but three are tutorial-tier (Titanic, Housing, MNIST) — every ML beginner has these exact three. Recruiters skimming GitHub will pattern-match you as "did a course" unless the RAG/FastAPI work is front and center. So pin only 4: RAG Study Assistant, FastAPI RAG API, NayePankh Chatbot, and MNIST (as your one "I understand deep learning fundamentals" proof). Drop Titanic and Housing from pins — keep them in repo but don't showcase.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Bio problem:</strong> you haven't given me one to fix. Send it, or I'm guessing. Generic MCA-student bios say "passionate about AI/ML" — cut that phrase if it's in there, it's noise every recruiter skips.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Profile README — what actually works for 8-12 LPA off-campus:</strong><br>
Not "Hi, I'm Rahul, aspiring ML engineer" intros. Recruiters at that level want: what have you shipped that works in production, not what you've learned. Structure:</p>

<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">That last section is the one recruiters at good startups actually notice — it separates "followed a tutorial" from "understands tradeoffs." Most candidates skip it. Don't.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>What I need from you to finish this properly:</strong></p>

<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Your current bio text</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Whether RAG Study Assistant and the FastAPI RAG API are the same project or different (your message is ambiguous — is the API the backend for the Streamlit app, or separate?)</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Second question matters — if they're the same system split into two repos, say that explicitly in both READMEs or it looks like <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Reality check:</strong></p>
<ul class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">3 repos total: <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Expanse-tracker</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">ai-engineer-journey</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">my-portfolio</code>. None of the 6 projects you listed (Titanic, Housing, MNIST, RAG Assistant, NayePankh, FastAPI RAG) are visible on this account.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Current bio: <em>"Tech Skills learner &amp; aspiring developer... Currently improving my coding skills"</em> — this actively undersells you if those projects are real. "Learner" and "aspiring" are the exact words that get filtered out at 8-12 LPA screening.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">No profile README exists yet (the special repo named exactly your username — <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Rahulgupta-63/Rahulgupta-63</code> — isn't there, that's what renders on the profile page).</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">1 follower, 0 following — irrelevant for hiring but confirms this is a fresh/unused account.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">You have a portfolio site linked: rahulgupta-63.github.io/my-portfolio — I haven't checked that yet.</li>
</ul>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Real question before I write anything:</strong> where are the 6 projects actually hosted? Different GitHub account, private repos, or not yet pushed? If they're not on this public GitHub, no README I write matters — recruiters click through and find 3 unrelated/empty-looking repos, which is worse than having nothing, because it looks like you're lying or disorganized.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Give me the actual repo URLs for the 6 projects, or confirm they need to be created/pushed here first.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-focus-within:opacity-100 pointer-coarse:opacity-100 transition"><div class="text-text-300"><div role="toolbar" aria-label="Message actions" class="text-text-300 flex select-none items-stretch justify-between"><button type="button" data-cds="Button" class="cds-reset group/btn relative isolate inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-1.5 whitespace-nowrap select-none cursor-[var(--cds-cursor-interactive)] aria-disabled:cursor-default data-[disabled]:cursor-default border-0 outline-none rounded h-control font-sans text-body font-medium [&amp;:disabled:not([aria-busy])]:opacity-50 disabled:pointer-events-none transition-shadow duration-fast focus-visible:shadow-focus text-primary aria-pressed:text-accent aspect-square w-control px-0 !text-muted hover:!text-primary" aria-label="Copy" data-testid="action-bar-copy" id="base-ui-_r_5hl_" tabindex="0"><span aria-hidden="true" class="absolute -z-[1] rounded-[inherit] transition-colors duration-fast group-focus-visible/btn:shadow-[inset_0_0_0_1px_var(--cds-page-bg)] bg-transparent group-hover/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover group-aria-expanded/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover inset-0 group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent group-hover/btn:group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent cds-btn-squish "></span><span class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 "><span class="relative"><span data-cds="Icon" class="transition-all opacity-100 scale-100" aria-hidden="true" style="font-family: var(--font-anthropicons, Anthropicons-Variable); <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Until I see actual contents, here's the blunt state of things:</p>

<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">4 repos exist. 0 match the names of your 6 claimed projects.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">If those projects are just notebooks buried inside <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">ai-engineer-journey</code> with no per-project structure, that's a real problem — recruiters won't dig through one mega-repo to find "MNIST CNN" or "RAG Study Assistant." Each serious project needs its own repo with its own README, or at minimum clearly separated folders with direct links from your profile README.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Expanse-tracker</code> and <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">my-portfolio</code> aren't on your original list of 6 — are these separate, or did your list skip them on purpose?</li>
</ul>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Send screenshots/paste of <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">ai-engineer-journey</code> contents. That decides whether we're restructuring repos first or just writing READMEs.</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>What's actually there:</strong><br>
Titanic, Housing, MNIST — all confirmed, but dumped as 4 loose .py/.ipynb files in one repo called "ai-engineer-journey," described as "MCA 1st Year student learning AI." No folders per project, one file literally named <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">tanderanalysis.ipynb.ipynb</code> (typo, double extension — looks careless, not the file itself, the naming).</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>What's missing entirely:</strong><br>
RAG Study Assistant, NayePankh Chatbot, FastAPI RAG API — none of these exist in this repo or in the other 3 repos (<code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Rahulgupta-63</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">my-portfolio</code>, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">Expanse-tracker</code>). These are your strongest projects and they're not on GitHub at all. If they're only on HuggingFace Spaces, a recruiter checking your GitHub sees zero LLM/RAG code — just three beginner tutorials.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Problem, ranked:</strong></p>
<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Your 3 best projects (RAG stuff) have no GitHub presence. This is the actual blocker, not README copy.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Repo framed as "learning journey," not "engineer." That label alone signals junior/tutorial-follower to anyone scanning fast.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">File named with a typo and double extension.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">"MCA 1st Year" in the About — if you're closer to graduating, this undersells you and may be outdated.</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Before I write any README:</strong> are the RAG/FastAPI/NayePankh projects pushed anywhere on GitHub under a different name, or do they only exist on HuggingFace right now? <li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Bio: "Tech Skills learner &amp; aspiring developer / Exploring Git, GitHub &amp; real-world projects / Currently improving my coding skills" — this contradicts what you showed me in the screenshot ("AI/ML Engineer | RAG, LLMs, PyTorch"). opacity: 1; transform: none;"><div class="contents"><div data-is-streaming="false" class="group relative relative pb-[var(--msg-assistant-pb,0.75rem)]" style="opacity: 1; transform: none;"><h2 data-find-omitted="" class="sr-only select-none">Claude responded: That bio is wrong, not just weak.</h2><div class="font-claude-response relative leading-[1.65rem] [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:bg-bg-000/50 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-0.5 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-border-400 [&amp;_.ignore-pre-bg&gt;div]:bg-transparent [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8"><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">That bio is wrong, not just weak.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">"AI/ML Engineer" — you're an MCA student with 3 tutorial repos on GitHub. Calling yourself Engineer before you have shipped code is a claim recruiters will check and fail. Undermines you the moment someone opens the repo tab.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">"RAG, LLMs" — no RAG code exists on your GitHub. If a recruiter reads this bio then clicks repos, the mismatch is worse than a boring bio. It reads as bluffing.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Fix bio only after fixing repos, not before. Right order:</p>
<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Confirm where RAG/NayePankh/FastAPI code actually lives — GitHub, private, or nowhere.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Push that code publicly first.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Then bio can honestly say "Building RAG systems" or similar — still not "Engineer."</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Answer the open question: where is the RAG code right now?</p></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" z-[2] min-w-0"><div style=""><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">That's the real problem. No GitHub code = recruiters can't verify skill. HF Spaces link alone isn't enough — most recruiters check GitHub first, not HF.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Fix order:</p>
<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Push RAG Assistant, NayePankh, FastAPI RAG code to GitHub as separate repos. Non-negotiable — "deployed but no repo" looks like you didn't write it yourself.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Each gets its own repo, own README (I'll write these once pushed).</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Then bio changes to something earned, not "Engineer."</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Question: do you have the code locally / in HF Spaces files right now, ready to push? Or is it lost/only in HF's managed environment?</p></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start opacity-0 style=""><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Plan is fine, but one gap: you said "code locally + HF" — confirm the actual files are on your laptop right now, not just deployed inside HF Spaces UI. If you only edited files inside HF's web editor, you need to clone from HF first before this works.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Step-by-step, one repo at a time:</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Step 1 — Get the code local</strong><br>
If it's on HF Spaces:</p>
<div role="group" aria-label="Code" tabindex="0" class="relative group/copy bg-bg-000/50 border-0.5 border-border-400 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent-100"><div class="sticky opacity-0 group-hover/copy:opacity-100 group-focus-within/copy:opacity-100 top-2 py-2 h-12 w-0 float-right"><div class="absolute right-0 h-8 px-2 items-center inline-flex z-10"><button class="inline-flex

<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">This pulls the actual files to your machine.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Step 2 — Clean it before pushing</strong><br>
Remove: API keys, <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.env</code> files with secrets, unnecessary cache/checkpoint files. Add a <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.gitignore</code>. Recruiters open code — leaked keys look worse than no repo.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Step 3 — Create new GitHub repo</strong><br>
Name it clearly: <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">rag-study-assistant</code>, not <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">project1</code>. New repo on GitHub, don't reuse <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">ai-engineer-journey</code>.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Step 4 — Push</strong></p>

</span></span><span>git remote set-url origin https://github.com/Rahulgupta-63/rag-study-assistant.git
</span><span>git push -u origin main</span></code></pre></div></div>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Step 5 — README</strong><br>
I write this once code is actually pushed and I can see file structure — not before, guessing at structure wastes both our time.</p>

<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Question before you start:</strong> do you have HF login access right now to clone, or is this blocked (private space, lost credentials, etc.)? <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">For each project folder:</p>

</span></span><span>git init
</span><span>git add .
</span><span>git commit -m "initial commit"
</span><span>git branch -M main
</span><span>git remote add origin https://github.com/Rahulgupta-63/rag-study-assistant.git
</span><span>git push -u origin main</span></code></pre></div></div>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">First create the empty repo on GitHub.com (name: <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">rag-study-assistant</code>) before running this — otherwise push fails.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">One question: does the folder have a <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.env</code> or any file with API keys (Groq key, etc.)? <p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Problems before you push anything:</p>
<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">rag-api-key.pem</code></strong> — this is a private key file. Never push this. Add to <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.gitignore</code> immediately.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong>PDFs and notes</strong> (docker.pdf, fastapi.pdf, cloudDeplyement.pdf, D3_Cloud_Deployment_Notes.md, L1_Variables_DataTypes.html) — these are study material, not project code. Don't push them. They make the repo look like a course folder, not a product.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">error.txt</code></strong> — drop it, it's a debug leftover.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong><code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">__pycache__</code></strong> — never push, always gitignore.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Two apps here: <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">app.py</code> (RAG assistant?) and <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">main.py</code> (FastAPI?) — these might actually be two separate projects mixed in one folder. Confirm: is <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">main.py</code> the FastAPI wrapper you mentioned as project #6?</li>
</ol>

Files listed there stay on your laptop but never go to GitHub.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Your list:</p>
<div role="group" aria-label="Code" tabindex="0" class="relative group/copy bg-bg-000/50 border-0.5 border-border-400 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent-100"><div class="sticky opacity-0 group-hover/copy:opacity-100 group-focus-within/copy:opacity-100 top-2 py-2 h-12 w-0 float-right"><div class="absolute right-0 h-8 px-2 items-center inline-flex z-10"><button class="inline-flex

          ease-[cubic-bezier(0.165,0.85,0.45,1)] h-8 w-8 rounded-md backdrop-blur-md _fill_10ocf_9 _ghost_10ocf_96" type="button" aria-label="Copy to clipboard" data-state="closed"><div class="relative"><div class="transition-all opacity-100 scale-100" style="width: 20px; height: 20px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="transition-all opacity-100 scale-100" aria-hidden="true" style="flex-shrink: 0;"><path d="M12.5 3A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 14 4.5V6h1.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 17 7.5v8a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5h-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 6 15.5V14H4.5A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 3 12.5v-8A1.5 1.5 0 0 1 4.5 3zm1.5 9.5a1.5 1.5 0 0 1-1.5 1.5H7v1.5a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5H14zM4.5 4a.5.5 0 0 0-.5.5v8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5.5h8a.5.5 0 0 0 .5-.5v-8a.5.5 0 0 0-.5-.5z"></path></svg></div><div class="absolute inset-0 flex items-center justify-center"><div class="transition-all opacity-0 scale-50" style="width: 20px; height: 20px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><svg width="20" height="20" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" class="transition-all opacity-0 scale-50" aria-hidden="true" style="flex-shrink: 0;"><path d="M15.188 5.11a.5.5 0 0 1 .752.626l-.056.084-7.5 9a.5.5 0 0 1-.738.033l-3.5-3.5-.064-.078a.501.501 0 0 1 .693-.693l.078.064 3.113 3.113 7.15-8.58z"></path></svg></div></div></div></button></div></div><div class="overflow-x-auto"><pre class="code-block__code !my-0 !rounded-lg !text-sm !leading-relaxed p-3.5" style="color: rgb(234, 236, 240); background: transparent; font-family: var(--font-mono);"><code style="color: rgb(234, 236, 240); background: transparent; font-family: var(--font-mono); white-space: pre-wrap;"><span><span>.env              → skips your API keys file
</span></span><span>__pycache__/      → skips Python's auto-generated cache folder
</span><span>*.pem             → skips your private key file
</span><span>*.pdf             → skips all PDF files (your notes)
</span><span>*.md              → skips markdown files (also skips README.md — problem, fix below)
</span><span>error.txt         → skips that one debug file</span></code></pre></div></div>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Bug: <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">*.md</code> will also block your README.md from being pushed later. Fix:</p>
<div role="group" aria-label="Code" tabindex="0" class="relative group/copy bg-bg-000/50 border-0.5 border-border-400 rounded-lg focus:outline-none focus-visible:ring-2 focus-visible:ring-accent-100"><div class="sticky opacity-0 group-hover/copy:opacity-100 group-focus-within/copy:opacity-100 top-2 py-2 h-12 w-0 float-right"><div class="absolute right-0 h-8 px-2 items-center inline-flex z-10"><button class="inline-flex
  items-center
  justify-center

  isolate

opacity: 1; transform: none;"><div class="contents"><div data-is-streaming="false" class="group relative relative pb-[var(--msg-assistant-pb,0.75rem)]" style="opacity: 1; transform: none;"><h2 data-find-omitted="" class="sr-only select-none">Claude responded: Two repos then.</h2><div class="font-claude-response relative leading-[1.65rem] [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:bg-bg-000/50 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-0.5 [&amp;_pre&gt;div]:border-border-400 [&amp;_.ignore-pre-bg&gt;div]:bg-transparent [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.standard-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pl-2 [&amp;_.progressive-markdown_:is(p,blockquote,ul,ol,h1,h2,h3,h4,h5,h6)]:pr-8"><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Two repos then. main.py imports from app.py — that's a dependency, not a merge reason. Keep separate:</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>repo 1: rag-study-assistant</strong> → app.py, Dockerfile, requirements.txt<br>
<strong>repo 2: rag-fastapi-service</strong> → main.py, its own Dockerfile, requirements.txt</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Problem: main.py imports from app.py. If they're separate repos, repo 2 breaks unless you either:</p>
<ul class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-disc flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">copy the needed function into repo 2, or</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">make repo 1 a pip-installable package repo 2 depends on (overkill for now)</li>
</ul>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Simplest fix: copy the specific function(s) main.py needs directly into repo 2's own file. Don't cross-import across repos.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Confirm: how many functions does main.py import from app.py? Number only.</p></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-focus-within:opacity-100 pointer-coarse:opacity-100 style=""><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Doesn't matter what you meant it for — app.py literally does <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">from text import Groq</code>. That's a real import. If text.py isn't in the repo, the code breaks for anyone who clones it. Intent doesn't fix code.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Two fixes, pick one:</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Fix A (proper):</strong> In app.py, replace <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">from text import Groq</code> with the correct import — probably <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">from groq import Groq</code> (the actual Groq SDK). Check if <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">groq</code> is in requirements.txt.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal"><strong>Fix B (quick):</strong> Just push text.py too.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Fix A is correct. Fix B ships a leftover test file to a "professional" repo — bad look, contradicts your goal.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Check requirements.txt:</p>

Good.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">But <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.gitignore</code> isn't in the list. That's a problem: if <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.gitignore</code> itself never got committed, it might not be tracked on GitHub, meaning future pushes could accidentally leak <code class="bg-text-200/5 border border-0.5 border-border-300 text-danger-000 whitespace-pre-wrap rounded-[0.4rem] px-1 py-px text-[0.9rem]">.env</code> again if someone re-clones and re-adds files without realizing the ignore rules aren't saved remotely.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Check:</p>

<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong>Chunk size / retrieval limitation</strong> — I guessed at this based on typical RAG setups. If your actual limitation is different (e.g. token limits, no reranking, whatever), tell me and I'll correct it. Don't ship a limitations section that's wrong.</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2"><strong>"No persistent storage across sessions"</strong> — confirm this is true. If ChromaDB is actually persisted (saved to disk/volume), this line is false and needs removing.</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Push:</p>

<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">But: this repo still isn't proof of skill by itself. It's a Streamlit app calling Groq's API with off-the-shelf embeddings — standard RAG tutorial pattern, not distinctive engineering. Fine as a working project, weak as a differentiator. Don't oversell it in your profile README as your flagship.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Next repos to push: NayePankh, FastAPI service. Which one now?</p></div></div></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-focus-within:opacity-100 pointer-coarse:opacity-100 transition"><div class="text-text-300"><div role="toolbar" aria-label="Message actions" class="text-text-300 flex select-none items-stretch justify-between"><button type="button" data-cds="Button" class="cds-reset group/btn relative isolate inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-1.5 whitespace-nowrap select-none cursor-[var(--cds-cursor-interactive)] aria-disabled:cursor-default data-[disabled]:cursor-default border-0 outline-none rounded h-control font-sans text-body font-medium [&amp;:disabled:not([aria-busy])]:opacity-50 disabled:pointer-events-none transition-shadow duration-fast focus-visible:shadow-focus text-primary aria-pressed:text-accent aspect-square w-control px-0 !text-muted hover:!text-primary" aria-label="Copy" data-testid="action-bar-copy" id="base-ui-_r_7op_" tabindex="0" fdprocessedid="ye8wq"><span aria-hidden="true" class="absolute -z-[1] rounded-[inherit] transition-colors duration-fast group-focus-visible/btn:shadow-[inset_0_0_0_1px_var(--cds-page-bg)] bg-transparent group-hover/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover group-aria-expanded/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover inset-0 group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent group-hover/btn:group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent cds-btn-squish "></span><span class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 "><span class="relative"><span data-cds="Icon" class="transition-all opacity-100 scale-100" aria-hidden="true" style="font-family: var(--font-anthropicons, Anthropicons-Variable); Bad for a professional profile, looks like a typo to anyone visiting.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Fix: rename on GitHub.com (Settings → repo name → remove leading dash). Don't create new repo, just rename — keeps commits intact.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">After rename, update remote:</p>

transform: none;"><div class="flex flex-row gap-2 relative"><div class="flex-1"><div data-testid="user-message" class="font-large !font-user-message py-0.5 grid grid-cols-1 gap-2 relative [&amp;_ul]:!space-y-0 [&amp;_ol]:!space-y-0 [&amp;_ul]:pl-8 [&amp;_ol]:pl-8" style="max-height: 200px; overflow: hidden; position: relative;"><p class="whitespace-pre-wrap break-words">ABOUT NAYEPANKH FOUNDATION
NayePankh Foundation is a UP Government registered NGO founded on 28th March 2021. It is one of the biggest student-led organizations in India, working towards uplifting the underprivileged. The foundation is registered under the Indian Society Act 1860, affiliated with NITI Aayog, and holds 80G and 12A certifications.
HOW IT STARTED
NayePankh Foundation was started during the COVID-19 pandemic in 2020 by a group of high school students who wanted to help the underprivileged. They started by distributing food and essential supplies during the lockdown. The foundation officially launched on 28th March 2021 and has since grown into a large team of volunteers from different parts of Uttar Pradesh.
WHAT NAYEPANKH MEANS
NayePankh means "new wings" â€” the foundation aims to give wings to the underprivileged sections of society and uplift them for a better future. Their motto is "BADALTE BHARAT KI NAYI</p><div class="absolute bottom-0 left-0 right-0 h-12 bg-gradient-to-t from-bg-300 to-transparent pointer-events-none"></div></div><button class="pb-3 pt-1 text-xs text-text-500/80 hover:text-text-100 transition w-3/4 text-left rounded-lg" fdprocessedid="7d9w">Show more</button></div></div></div><div data-message-action-bar="" class="flex justify-start opacity-0 group-hover:opacity-100 group-focus-within:opacity-100 pointer-coarse:opacity-100 transition"><div class="text-text-300"><div role="toolbar" aria-label="Message actions" class="text-text-300 flex select-none items-stretch justify-between"><span class="text-text-500 text-xs flex items-center mr-2" id="base-ui-_r_9nm_">17:03</span><div class="flex items-center"><button type="button" data-cds="Button" class="cds-reset group/btn relative isolate inline-flex shrink-0 items-center justify-center gap-1.5 whitespace-nowrap select-none cursor-[var(--cds-cursor-interactive)] aria-disabled:cursor-default data-[disabled]:cursor-default border-0 outline-none rounded h-control font-sans text-body font-medium [&amp;:disabled:not([aria-busy])]:opacity-50 disabled:pointer-events-none transition-shadow duration-fast focus-visible:shadow-focus text-primary aria-pressed:text-accent aspect-square w-control px-0 !text-muted hover:!text-primary" aria-label="Retry" id="base-ui-_r_9r2_" tabindex="-1"><span aria-hidden="true" class="absolute -z-[1] rounded-[inherit] transition-colors duration-fast group-focus-visible/btn:shadow-[inset_0_0_0_1px_var(--cds-page-bg)] bg-transparent group-hover/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover group-aria-expanded/btn:bg-fill-ghost-hover inset-0 group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent group-hover/btn:group-aria-pressed/btn:bg-accent cds-btn-squish "></span><span class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 "><span data-cds="Icon" aria-hidden="true" style="font-family: var(--font-anthropicons, Anthropicons-Variable); Safe to keep pushed.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Writing README now.</p></div></div><div class="mt-4"><div class="grid grid-rows-[auto_auto] min-w-0"><div class="row-start-1 col-start-1 min-w-0"><div data-find-omitted="" class="contents"><div class="min-w-0 pl-2 py-[var(--msg-pill-py,0.375rem)]"><div class="flex items-center gap-2"><button class="group/status flex items-center gap-2 py-1 text-sm transition-colors cursor-pointer text-left text-text-500 hover:text-text-300 flex-1 min-w-0" aria-expanded="false" fdprocessedid="r1oo15"><div class="inline-flex items-center gap-1 min-w-0" style="opacity: 1;"><span class="truncate font-base">Created a file, read a file</span><span class="inline-flex -rotate-90 shrink-0 opacity-0 transition-opacity duration-200 group-hover/status:opacity-100 group-focus-visible/status:opacity-100"><div style="width: 12px; height: 12px; display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: center;"><svg width="16" height="16" viewBox="0 0 20 20" fill="currentColor" xmlns="http://www.w3.org/2000/svg" aria-hidden="true" style="flex-shrink: 0;"><path d="M14.128 7.165a.502.502 0 0 1 .744.67l-4.5 5-.078.07a.5.5 0 0 1-.666-.07l-4.5-5-.06-.082a.501.501 0 0 1 .729-.656l.075.068L10 11.752z"></path></svg></div></span></div></button></div><span class="sr-only" role="status" aria-live="polite">Created a file, read a file</span><div class="grid transition-[grid-template-rows] duration-300 ease-out" style="grid-template-rows: 0fr;"><div class="overflow-hidden min-w-0"></div></div></div></div></div><div class="row-start-2 col-start-1 relative grid grid-rows-[auto_auto] isolate min-w-0"><div class="row-start-1 col-start-1 relative z-[2] min-w-0"><div style=""><div><div class="standard-markdown grid-cols-1 grid [&amp;_&gt;_*]:min-w-0 gap-3 standard-markdown"><p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Weak point in this README, not hiding it: I added a "Note" saying it was a hiring task, not a production deployment. That's honest, but it might read as "I built this to pass a test, not because anyone's using it" — slightly weaker positioning than the RAG assistant repo. Keep it though — lying by omission is worse if someone asks in an interview.</p>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Two things you need to fill in before pushing:</p>
<ol class="[li_&amp;]:mb-0 [li_&amp;]:mt-1 [li_&amp;]:gap-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ul]:pb-1 [&amp;:not(:last-child)_ol]:pb-1 list-decimal flex flex-col gap-1 pl-8 mb-3">
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">HF Spaces link — I left a placeholder</li>
<li class="font-claude-response-body whitespace-normal break-words pl-2">Confirm chatbot has no conversation memory — I assumed this from the code you showed (no session state visible). Correct me if wrong.</li>
</ol>
<p class="font-claude-response-body break-words whitespace-normal">Push:</p>
